docs.title: Asyncio
docs.text:
async func could be await-ed in jupyter lab directly:

import asyncio
import websockets
import datetime

async def attach(uri, n=5):
    data = []
    async with websockets.connect(uri) as ws:
        for i in range(n):
            recv = await ws.recv()
            print(recv)
            data.append(recv)
    return data

data = await attach(endpoint)
len(data)

Binance

docs.title: Debian
docs.text:
turn off

sudo systemctl reboot
sudo systemctl shutdown

view log ssh

journalctl -t sshd

docs.title: Minikube
docs.text:
set up k8s in debian

install docker via: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/debian/

add current user to docker user:

sudo usermod -aG docker $USER && newgrp docker

install https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/start/?arch=%2Flinux%2Fx86-64%2Fstable%2Fdebian+package

start minikube

minikube start

run kubectl from minikube

minikube kubectl -- get pods -A

alias kubectl="minikube kubectl --"

dashboard

minikube dashboard
